hooks.description: Detect iOS project and cache metadata for context-aware hooks

hooks.command: INPUT=$(cat); if echo "$INPUT" | grep -qE 'git\s+push.*(-f|--force).*(main|master)'; then echo '🚫 Dangerous: force push to main/master rewrites shared history. Use --force-with-lease on a feature branch or push to a new branch.' && exit 2; fi; if echo "$INPUT" | grep -qE 'xcrun\s+simctl\s+erase\s+all'; then echo '⚠️  xcrun simctl erase all wipes ALL simulator state. Prefer erase <device-id> for a single device.' && exit 1; fi; exit 0
hooks.description: Block dangerous Bash operations (force push to main, simctl erase all)

hooks.command: INPUT=$(cat); if echo "$INPUT" | grep -qE 'xcodebuild\s+.*archive'; then SCRIPT="${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/validate_pre_archive.sh"; if [ -f "$SCRIPT" ]; then bash "$SCRIPT"; exit $?; fi; fi; exit 0
hooks.description: Pre-archive validator — runs validate_pre_archive.sh before xcodebuild archive

hooks.command: EXT="${CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH##*.}"; CACHE="$(pwd)/.ios-from-web-guide-project.json"; if [ "$EXT" = "swift" ] || [ "$EXT" = "plist" ] || [ "$(basename "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH")" = "project.yml" ]; then if [ -f "$CACHE" ]; then IS_IOS=$(grep -o '"is_ios_project":\s*[a-z]*' "$CACHE" | grep -o '[a-z]*$'); [ "$IS_IOS" = "false" ] && exit 0; fi; echo '💡 Reminder: Did you invoke the relevant ios-from-web-guide skill before writing this file? If not, invoke it now and verify your code follows the rules.'; fi; exit 0
hooks.description: Remind about skill invocation for iOS files (context-aware)

hooks.command: EXT="${CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH##*.}"; [ "$EXT" != "swift" ] && exit 0; if grep -qE 'UserDefaults.*\b(token|password|secret|api_key|bearer)\b' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then echo '🚫 Storing secrets in UserDefaults detected.' && echo '' && echo '   💡 Fix: Use KeychainService.save(token:) / .getToken() / .deleteToken(). Never UserDefaults for secrets.' && echo '' && echo '   See: ios-auth-keychain-storage skill' && exit 2; fi; exit 0
hooks.description: Block UserDefaults storage of tokens/passwords/secrets — use Keychain

hooks.command: EXT="${CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH##*.}"; [ "$EXT" != "swift" ] && exit 0; if grep -A 20 'NavigationLink(value:' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'buttonStyle(.plain)'; then echo '🚫 .buttonStyle(.plain) inside NavigationLink detected.' && echo '' && echo '   💡 Fix: Use .buttonStyle(.borderless) inside NavigationLink. .plain only strips styling; .borderless claims tap ownership so the NavigationLink fires correctly.' && echo '' && echo '   See: swiftui-navigation-foundations skill' && exit 2; fi; exit 0

EXT="${CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH##*.}"; [ "$EXT" != "swift" ] && exit 0; if grep -qE 'URL\(string:\s*"\/[^h]' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then echo '⚠️  URL(string:) with a relative path — this returns nil.' && echo '' && echo '   💡 Fix: Use .asBackendURL on the string. Relative paths resolve to nil in URL(string:).' && echo '' && echo '   See: swiftui-async-image-with-backend-paths skill' && exit 1; fi; exit 0
hooks.description: Warn on URL(string:) with relative path — use .asBackendURL

hooks.command: EXT="${CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH##*.}"; [ "$EXT" != "swift" ] && exit 0; COLLAPSED=$(tr '\n' ' ' < "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null); if echo "$COLLAPSED" | grep -qE '@MainActor[^{]*(class|struct|actor)[^{]*\{[^}]*DispatchQueue\.main\.async'; then echo '⚠️  DispatchQueue.main.async inside @MainActor is redundant.' && echo '' && echo '   💡 Fix: Remove it. All methods on @MainActor already run on the main actor.' && exit 1; fi; exit 0

EXT="${CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH##*.}"; [ "$EXT" != "swift" ] && exit 0; CACHE="$(pwd)/.ios-from-web-guide-project.json"; [ -f "$CACHE" ] || exit 0; TARGET=$(grep -o '"deployment_target":\s*"[^"]*"' "$CACHE" | sed -E 's/.*"([0-9.]+)".*/\1/'); MAJOR=$(echo "$TARGET" | cut -d. -f1); [ -z "$MAJOR" ] && exit 0; if [ "$MAJOR" -lt 17 ] 2>/dev/null; then exit 0; fi; if grep -qE '@Published|@ObservedObject|@StateObject' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then echo '⚠️  @Published/@ObservedObject/@StateObject on iOS 17+ — prefer @Observable.' && echo '' && echo '   💡 Fix: Use @Observable (Swift Observation framework). iOS 17+ makes the old ObservableObject pattern obsolete.' && echo '' && echo '   See: swiftui-observable-viewmodel-boilerplate skill' && exit 1; fi; exit 0
hooks.description: Warn on @Published/@ObservedObject/@StateObject in iOS 17+ Observable projects

hooks.command: EXT="${CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH##*.}"; [ "$EXT" != "swift" ] && exit 0; if echo "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" | grep -qE '_test\.swift$|/Tests/|/SnapshotTests/'; then exit 0; fi; if grep -qE '^\s*print\(' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then echo '⚠️  print() call outside test files — shouldnt ship.' && echo '' && echo '   💡 Fix: Replace print() with os.Logger for production logging.' && echo '' && echo '     import os' && echo '     let logger = Logger(subsystem: "com.example.app", category: "feature")' && echo '     logger.info("message")' && exit 1; fi; exit 0
hooks.description: Warn on print() outside test files — use os.Logger

hooks.command: EXT="${CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH##*.}"; [ "$EXT" != "swift" ] && exit 0; COLLAPSED=$(tr '\n' ' ' < "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null); if echo "$COLLAPSED" | grep -qE 'struct\s+[A-Z][A-Za-z0-9_]*\s*:[^{]*Hashable'; then BODY=$(echo "$COLLAPSED" | grep -oE 'static func ==[^{]*\{[^}]*\}' | head -1); if [ -n "$BODY" ]; then if echo "$BODY" | grep -q 'lhs\.id\s*==\s*rhs\.id' && ! echo "$BODY" | grep -q '&&'; then echo '⚠️  Custom id-only `==` detected on a Hashable struct — this breaks SwiftUI diffing.' && echo '' && echo '   💡 Fix: Remove the custom `==` and let Swift auto-synthesize structural equality:' && echo '' && echo '     struct Post: Hashable { ... }  // no custom == needed' && echo '' && echo '   If you need identity-only equality for navigation routing, wrap the id in a separate Identifiable-only type.' && echo '' && echo '   See: swiftui-equatable-hashable-for-diffing skill' && exit 1; fi; fi; fi; exit 0
hooks.description: Warn on custom id-only == on Hashable struct (breaks SwiftUI diffing)

hooks.command: EXT="${CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH##*.}"; if [ "$EXT" != "swift" ]; then exit 0; fi; if echo "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" | grep -qE '/Views/'; then echo '💡 Consider running the swiftui-checklist agent on this file to catch common pitfalls (navigation, state, equality, layout).'; fi; exit 0
hooks.description: Post-write: suggest dispatching swiftui-checklist agent on Views/ .swift files

hooks.command:
printf '%s\n' '## iOS From Web Guide — Rules for Subagents

You are working in an iOS/SwiftUI project. Follow these rules strictly.

### SwiftUI Navigation
1. Use `.buttonStyle(.borderless)` on Buttons inside a NavigationLink, never `.plain`
2. Hoist `navigationDestination(for:)` to the root view that owns the NavigationStack
3. Prefer `NavigationLink(value:)` + `navigationDestination(for:)` over `NavigationLink(destination:)` (deprecated)

### State Management
1. Use `@Observable` (iOS 17+). Never `@Published`/`@ObservedObject`/`@StateObject`
2. Let Swift auto-synthesize Hashable. Never write a custom id-only `==` on a model struct
3. In Views, use `@State private var viewModel = FooViewModel()` for Observable classes

### API / Auth
1. Store tokens in Keychain (KeychainService). Never UserDefaults
2. Use snake_case on the wire — `.convertToSnakeCase` / `.convertFromSnakeCase` on JSONEncoder/Decoder
3. Always resolve relative paths via `.asBackendURL` — never `URL(string: relativePath)`
4. APIClient handles base URL internally; call sites pass relative paths

### Shipping
1. `CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION` must strictly increment between TestFlight uploads
2. App icon must be RGB, no alpha channel
3. Every import of PhotosUI/AVFoundation/CoreLocation/ATT requires its Usage Description in Info.plist
4. Keep `DEVELOPMENT_TEAM` in project.yml, not Xcode (XcodeGen wipes Xcode-only edits)

### Layout
1. Use `.containerRelativeFrame(.horizontal, alignment: .leading)` to pin width in ScrollView. `.frame(maxWidth: .infinity)` accepts infinity but does not cap'; exit 0
hooks.description: Inject iOS from Web Guide rules into every subagent
